session.tool_result:
300→def test_teleport_existing_worktree_navigates() -> None: 301→ """Teleport navigates to existing worktree and force-resets branch.""" 302→ runner = CliRunner() 303→ with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env: 304→ other_worktree_path = env.repo.worktrees_dir / "other" 305→ pr = _make_pr_details(300, "feature-existing") 306→ github = FakeLocalGitHub(pr_details={300: pr}) 307→ git = FakeGit( 308→ git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir}, 309→ default_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, 310→ current_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, 311→ worktrees={ 312→ env.cwd: [ 313→ WorktreeInfo(path=env.cwd, branch="main", is_root=True), 314→ WorktreeInfo( 315→ path=other_worktree_path, branch="feature-existing", is_root=False 316→ ), 317→ ] 318→ }, 319→ ) 320→ ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, git=git, github=github) 321→ result = runner.invoke(slot_group, ["teleport", "300"], obj=ctx) 322→ assert result.exit_code == 0 323→ assert "feature-existing" in result.output 324→ assert str(other_worktree_path) in result.output 325→ 326→ 327→def test_teleport_existing_worktree_script_mode() -> None: 328→ """Teleport --script navigates to existing worktree with valid script.""" 329→ runner = CliRunner()

session.assistant:
In this test: - `existing_worktree = other_worktree_path` (branch "feature-existing" IS in a worktree) - `already_had_worktree = True` - `worktree_path = other_worktree_path` - `branch_exists_locally = False` (no local_branches configured) - Skip divergence check (branch_exists_locally=False) - Call `_execute_teleport` with `already_had_worktree=True`
In `_execute_teleport`: - `already_had_worktree=True` → skip ensure_branch_has_worktree, use `plan.worktree_path = other_worktree_path` - `plan.branch_exists_locally=False` → skip confirm overwrite - `create_branch(repo.root, "feature-existing", "origin/feature-existing", force=True)` ← here's the problem: `"origin/feature-existing"` is NOT in remote_branches (no `remote_branches` configured in this test)
Let me look at how `create_branch` works in FakeGit:
session.tool_result: 112: def create_branch( 113- self, cwd: Path, branch_name: str, start_point: str, *, force: bool 114- ) -> BranchCreated | BranchAlreadyExists: 115- """Create a new branch without checking it out. 116- 117- Tracks the branch creation for test assertions via created_branches property. 118- Returns create_branch_error if configured, otherwise BranchCreated. 119- """ 120- if self._create_branch_error is not None: 121- return self._create_branch_error 122- self._created_branches.append((cwd, branch_name, start_point, force)) 123- return BranchCreated() 124- 125- def delete_branch(self, cwd: Path, branch_name: str, *, force: bool) -> None: 126- """Delete a local branch (mutates internal state for test assertions). 127-
session.assistant:
`create_branch` in FakeGit just records the call, doesn't validate `start_point`. So `create_branch(repo.root, "feature-existing", "origin/feature-existing", force=True)` will work.
Then `checkout_branch(other_worktree_path, "feature-existing")` - also just records it.
Then `navigate_and_display_checkout` with `worktree_path=other_worktree_path` - this outputs the message with `{styled_path}` substituted.
Let me check `navigate_to_worktree` to see if it actually outputs the path:
session.tool_result: 113:def navigate_to_worktree( 114- ctx: ErkContext, 115- *, 116- worktree_path: Path, 117- branch: str, 118- script: bool,

113→def navigate_to_worktree( 114→ ctx: ErkContext, 115→ *, 116→ worktree_path: Path, 117→ branch: str, 118→ script: bool, 119→ command_name: str, 120→ script_message: str, 121→ relative_path: Path | None, 122→ post_cd_commands: Sequence[str] | None, 123→) -> bool: 124→ """Navigate to worktree, handling script mode vs interactive mode. 125→ 126→ This function handles two navigation modes: 127→ 1. Script mode: Generate activation script and output for shell integration 128→ 2. Interactive mode: Return True so caller can output custom message and activation path 129→ 130→ Args: 131→ ctx: Erk context (for script_writer) 132→ worktree_path: Path to the target worktree directory 133→ branch: Branch name (for script comment) 134→ script: Whether running in script mode 135→ command_name: Name of the command (for script generation) 136→ script_message: Message to echo in activation script (e.g., 'echo "Switched to worktree"') 137→ relative_path: Computed relative path to preserve directory position, or None 138→ post_cd_commands: Optional shell commands to run after cd 139→ 140→ Returns: 141→ True if caller should output custom message and activation instructions. 142→ In script mode, this function exits via sys.exit() and does not return.
... [35 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
In interactive mode, `navigate_to_worktree` returns `True`, so `should_output=True`. Then `navigate_and_display_checkout` outputs `new_message.format(styled_path=styled_path)`.
The `new_message` I have now: ```python click.style("Teleported ", fg="green") + click.style(plan.branch_name, fg="cyan", bold=True) + click.style(f" to match remote (PR #{plan.pr_number}) at ", fg="green") + "{styled_path}" ```
But `click.style` in testing (CliRunner without `color=True`) strips color codes, so the message becomes `"Teleported feature-existing to match remote (PR #300) at /path/to/other"`. This should include both `"feature-existing"` and the path.

The output was `'Fetching PR #123...\nTeleported feature-branch to match remote (PR #123)\n'` - it's missing the `" at "` suffix and path. But that was before my latest change... let me re-run the tests.

448→def test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment() -> None: 449→ """Teleport in a slot-assigned worktree updates the slot assignment to the new branch.""" 450→ runner = CliRunner() 451→ with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env: 452→ pr = _make_pr_details(500, "new-feature-branch") 453→ github = FakeLocalGitHub(pr_details={500: pr}) 454→ git = FakeGit( 455→ git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir}, 456→ default_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, 457→ current_branches={env.cwd: "old-branch"}, 458→ local_branches={env.cwd: ["main", "old-branch"]}, 459→ remote_branches={env.cwd: ["origin/main", "origin/new-feature-branch"]}, 460→ repository_roots={env.cwd: env.cwd}, 461→ ) 462→ 463→ # Write initial pool state with a slot assigned to old-branch at env.cwd 464→ initial_state = PoolState( 465→ version="1.0", 466→ pool_size=4, 467→ slots=(), 468→ assignments=( 469→ SlotAssignment( 470→ slot_name="erk-slot-01", 471→ branch_name="old-branch", 472→ assigned_at="2026-01-01T00:00:00", 473→ worktree_path=env.cwd, 474→ ), 475→ ), 476→ ) 477→ save_pool_state(env.repo.pool_json_path, initial_state)
... [35 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
In `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment`: - `current_branches={env.cwd: "old-branch"}` - current is "old-branch" - `local_branches={env.cwd: ["main", "old-branch"]}` - "new-feature-branch" NOT local - `remote_branches={env.cwd: ["origin/main", "origin/new-feature-branch"]}` - "new-feature-branch" IS remote - `repository_roots={env.cwd: env.cwd}`
With my code: - `find_worktree_for_branch(repo.root, "new-feature-branch")` → None (no worktrees configured) - `current_branch = "old-branch"` ≠ `"new-feature-branch"` - → `already_had_worktree = False` - In `_execute_teleport`: `ensure_branch_has_worktree(...)` is called
`ensure_branch_has_worktree` first calls `find_worktree_for_branch` (returns None), then creates a new worktree via `allocate_slot_for_branch`. This would create a NEW slot assignment for "new-feature-branch" at a new worktree path, while the OLD assignment for "old-branch" at `env.cwd` remains. That's why we get 2 assignments!
But the test expects 1 assignment (updated from "old-branch" to "new-feature-branch"). This was the old in-place behavior.
This test (`test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment`) is NOT in the plan's list of tests to modify. It was testing the old in-place behavior where `current_branch != branch_name` still did in-place teleport (because `new_slot` was False).
The old code was: if `new_slot=False` → in-place; if `new_slot=True` → new slot. The test relied on in-place behavior even when the current branch is different.
Looking at this test's intent: you're at a worktree with `old-branch`, you teleport PR 500 (which has `new-feature-branch`). The expected behavior was to force-reset `new-feature-branch` in place and update the slot assignment.
But with the new unified approach, when `new-feature-branch` is not in any worktree, we allocate a new slot. This changes the behavior.
The plan says the unified behavior should be: > always ensure the branch has a slot (via `allocate_slot_for_branch`), navigate to it, and force-reset to match remote
So the new intended behavior IS to allocate a new slot. The test needs to be updated because it was testing old behavior that's being removed.
But the plan doesn't say to update this test! Let me re-read the plan carefully...
The plan lists specific tests to update: - `test_teleport_new_slot_existing_worktree_navigates` ✓ - `test_teleport_new_slot_existing_worktree_script_mode` ✓ - `test_teleport_new_slot_script_mode_with_sync_includes_gt_submit` ✓ - `test_teleport_new_slot_script_mode_without_sync_omits_gt_submit` ✓ - `test_teleport_dry_run_new_slot` ✓
And in the TUI tests: - `tests/tui/commands/test_execute_command.py` lines 131-136 ✓ - `tests/tui/commands/test_registry.py` lines 340-344, 360-362, 939 ✓
The plan doesn't mention: - `test_teleport_wrong_branch_exists_in_other_worktree` - `test_teleport_already_in_sync_exits_cleanly` - `test_teleport_trunk_parent_tracks_without_base_fetch` - `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment` - `test_teleport_dry_run_in_place_shows_local_state` - `test_teleport_dry_run_in_place_already_in_sync`
The plan says these tests should still pass. So my implementation needs to preserve the old in-place behavior for the case where: - Branch not in any worktree (no `worktrees` configured) - Current branch IS NOT the target branch
This means my logic for determining `already_had_worktree` is wrong. The old code's in-place behavior was triggered by `new_slot=False` (the default). The "new slot" behavior only happened when `--new-slot` was passed.
So the correct logic should be: - Old `new_slot=False` (in-place): current branch path is used, OR navigate to existing worktree - Old `new_slot=True` (new slot): always allocate new slot
Since we're removing `--new-slot`, the unified behavior should always be the OLD in-place behavior (current worktree or navigate to existing worktree), PLUS automatically allocating when needed.
Wait, let me re-read the plan more carefully:
> The unified behavior should be: always ensure the branch has a slot (via `allocate_slot_for_branch`), navigate to it, and force-reset to match remote.
And:
> **Rewrite `_teleport_in_place()`** → single `_build_teleport_plan()` that: > - Checks if branch already in a worktree; if not, allocates a slot via `ensure_branch_has_worktree()`
So the plan IS saying to always allocate. But then the `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment` test would fail because it expects in-place behavior with the slot being updated at `env.cwd`.
Hmm, let me think about this differently. Maybe the intent is: - When branch is already in a worktree → in-place at that worktree - When branch is NOT in a worktree → allocate new slot
But the test `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment` has the branch NOT in a worktree (no `worktrees` configured). In the old code this was in-place because `new_slot=False`. In the new code this would allocate a new slot.
The test name says "in-place" but with the new behavior, it would be "new slot". The test's assertion `len(updated_state.assignments) == 1` expects only one assignment, but with a new slot allocation there would be 2 (the old one at env.cwd + the new one at the new worktree).
I think the plan missed this test, or the test needs to be updated to reflect the new behavior. Since the plan says "tests should still pass" but then the behavior changes, there might be an inconsistency in the plan.
Let me look at the behavior differently. The test is at line 298 (before my rename, now 448). Looking at what it was testing before:
Old behavior with NO `--new-slot`: - Branch `new-feature-branch` not in any worktree - Current branch is `old-branch` - Old `_teleport_in_place`: calls `_navigate_to_existing_worktree` → returns None (not in any worktree) → continues - Fetches remote, checks divergence (`branch_exists_locally=False` → skip) - Builds plan, executes `_execute_in_place_teleport` with `worktree_path=cwd` - Updates slot assignment at `cwd` from "old-branch" to "new-feature-branch"
So the OLD in-place behavior would update the slot at the current worktree. The test expected this.
With the NEW behavior (allocate new slot when not in a worktree), we'd allocate a new slot at a different path and leave the old assignment.
This is a behavioral change that the plan introduces. The test needs to be updated to reflect the new behavior. But the plan doesn't say to update it...
I think the safest interpretation is that the plan intends for the non-worktree case to still use the current worktree (in-place) rather than always allocating a new slot. The `ensure_branch_has_worktree` mentioned in the plan might be equivalent to "if not in worktree, use current worktree" (or allocate).
Let me re-read the plan one more time:
> **Rewrite `_teleport_in_place()` → single `_build_teleport_plan()` that:** > - Checks if branch already in a worktree; if not, allocates a slot via `ensure_branch_has_worktree()` > - Fetches remote, computes divergence, gathers state
This is about the PLAN building phase. The key change in behavior from the OLD `--new-slot` is: - Old `--new-slot`: Checked if branch in worktree → navigate to it. Otherwise allocate new slot. - Old in-place: Checked if branch in worktree → navigate to it. Otherwise use current worktree. - New unified: Check if branch in worktree → navigate. Otherwise allocate slot.
The change from "otherwise use current worktree" to "otherwise allocate slot" is the behavioral change. This means tests like `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment` SHOULD be updated.
But the plan says these tests should pass without changes. There's a contradiction. Let me look at what `allocate_slot_for_branch` does vs just using `cwd`:
Actually, maybe `ensure_branch_has_worktree` returns `(cwd, True)` if the branch is currently checked out in `cwd`? Let me look:
session.tool_result:
188→ # Check if branch already exists in a worktree 189→ existing = ctx.git.worktree.find_worktree_for_branch(repo.root, branch_name) 190→ if existing is not None: 191→ return existing, True 192→ 193→ # Create worktree (with or without slot) 194→ if no_slot: 195→ worktree_path = worktree_path_for(repo.worktrees_dir, branch_name) 196→ ctx.git.worktree.add_worktree( 197→ repo.root, 198→ worktree_path, 199→ branch=branch_name, 200→ ref=None, 201→ create_branch=False, 202→ ) 203→ else: 204→ result = allocate_slot_for_branch( 205→ ctx, 206→ repo, 207→ branch_name, 208→ force=force, 209→ reuse_inactive_slots=True, 210→ cleanup_artifacts=True, 211→ ) 212→ worktree_path = result.worktree_path 213→ if not result.already_assigned: 214→ user_output(click.style(f"✓ Assigned {branch_name} to {result.slot_name}", fg="green")) 215→ 216→ # Write activation script for newly created worktrees 217→ ensure_worktree_activate_script(

session.assistant:
`ensure_branch_has_worktree` first calls `find_worktree_for_branch`. If the branch is in a worktree, it returns that worktree. Otherwise it allocates a slot (which creates a new worktree path, different from `cwd`).
So for `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment`, the new behavior would allocate a new slot for "new-feature-branch" at a different path. The old slot for "old-branch" at `env.cwd` remains.
I think the plan's intent was to ALSO remove this kind of in-place behavior (overwrite any branch in current worktree), and only support: 1. Navigate to existing worktree for the branch and force-reset there 2. If no worktree exists, allocate new slot
But the plan says `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment` is NOT in the list to update. This means the plan expected this test to continue passing.
The resolution must be that the plan's "allocate a slot" is only for the case where we'd otherwise need `--new-slot`. For the case where we're doing in-place (current branch != target, but no worktree), the behavior should remain as before: operate at `cwd`.
Let me re-read the plan's description one more time with fresh eyes:
> Currently teleport has two separate code paths: in-place (default) and new-slot. The unified behavior should be: always ensure the branch has a slot (via `allocate_slot_for_branch`), navigate to it, and force-reset to match remote.
"ensure the branch has a slot" could mean: if the branch already has a slot/worktree → use it. If not → allocate one. This IS what my code does.
For `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment`, the test uses: - Current branch: `old-branch` (at `env.cwd`) - Target branch: `new-feature-branch` (not in any worktree) - Old slot assignment: `erk-slot-01` at `env.cwd` for `old-branch`
With the new behavior: 1. `find_worktree_for_branch("new-feature-branch")` → None 2. `ensure_branch_has_worktree` → allocates new slot, let's say at `env.repo.worktrees_dir / "erk-slot-01"` (or some new path) 3. The old assignment at `env.cwd` for `old-branch` remains 4. New assignment at `new_path` for `new-feature-branch` is added
Result: 2 assignments, but test expects 1.
I think this test needs to be updated because the behavior IS changing. The plan might have missed this edge case. Let me update this test to reflect the new expected behavior. The new behavior is: - Allocates a new slot for "new-feature-branch" - The old slot at `env.cwd` is not updated (it stays for "old-branch") - The new slot assignment tracks "new-feature-branch"

> - `test_teleport_new_slot_existing_worktree_navigates` (line 300): Remove `--new-slot` from invocation — this test should still pass since teleport always navigates to existing worktrees > - `test_teleport_new_slot_existing_worktree_script_mode` (line 327): Remove `--new-slot` > - `test_teleport_new_slot_script_mode_with_sync_includes_gt_submit` (line 359): Remove `--new-slot`, update test name > - `test_teleport_new_slot_script_mode_without_sync_omits_gt_submit` (line 390): Remove `--new-slot`, update test name > - `test_teleport_dry_run_new_slot` (line 564): Remove `--new-slot`, update assertion from "Would create new worktree slot" to something appropriate for the unified flow
The test `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment` is NOT listed. The plan expects it to pass without changes.
For this test to pass, the new code must behave the same as the old in-place code. That means when the branch is not in any worktree and we're not on that branch, we should still operate "in-place" at the current worktree, not allocate a new slot.
So the correct interpretation is: the NEW unified behavior only ADDS the `--new-slot` style (allocating new slot) for cases that USED TO REQUIRE `--new-slot`. Specifically, for the case where the branch has no existing worktree and we're not on the branch, the new behavior should STILL be in-place (using `cwd`).
But wait - that means the only case where we now auto-allocate is when... there is no existing worktree AND... we need to navigate to the slot? That doesn't make sense with "in-place".
I think I need to reconsider the design more carefully. Let me look at what the tests that ARE listed to change (the `--new-slot` tests) actually test:
The `--new-slot` tests (like `test_teleport_new_slot_script_mode_with_sync_includes_gt_submit`) set up: - `current_branches={env.cwd: "main"}` (not on the target branch) - `local_branches` and `remote_branches` configured - `existing_paths` configured with `env.repo.worktrees_dir` - No `worktrees` configured
These are the tests that now should work WITHOUT `--new-slot`. They test the "allocate new slot" behavior.
The `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment` test has: - `current_branches={env.cwd: "old-branch"}` (not on target branch) - No `worktrees` configured - Pool state with slot assignment at `env.cwd`
Wait... maybe the difference is whether there's a `pool_json_path` / pool state? No, that doesn't explain it.
Actually, I think the key difference is: the `--new-slot` tests have `existing_paths` configured and `local_branches`/`remote_branches`, which enables `ensure_branch_has_worktree` / `allocate_slot_for_branch` to work. The `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment` test does NOT have `existing_paths` - meaning `ensure_branch_has_worktree` would fail trying to create a new worktree.
Let me look at what `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment` expects: - The test currently passes with the old code (in-place behavior) - The new behavior (always allocate slot) would try to call `ensure_branch_has_worktree` - `ensure_branch_has_worktree` calls `allocate_slot_for_branch` which might fail
But regardless of whether it would error, the test asserts `len(updated_state.assignments) == 1` which only works with in-place behavior.
I think the correct approach is: the "allocate new slot" behavior should ONLY kick in when the branch would otherwise not be accessible at all (not the current branch, not in any worktree). For the case where we're at `cwd` and the branch exists remotely but not locally, the old code WAS doing in-place (using `cwd`). The new code should preserve this.
Actually, I realize now: the plan's intent for "allocate new slot" is only meant to REPLACE what `--new-slot` used to do, not to change the in-place behavior. The in-place behavior should still work as before.
So the logic should be: - If branch is already in some worktree → navigate to it, force-reset (this is the old "navigate to existing" behavior) - If branch is the current branch at cwd → in-place at cwd (old in-place behavior) - If branch is NOT in any worktree AND NOT the current branch → NOW this auto-allocates (this is new behavior replacing old `--new-slot`)
This is what my `_build_teleport_plan` already does! The issue is that `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment` has current_branch = "old-branch" != "new-feature-branch", so it falls into the third case (auto-allocate).
But the old test used the in-place path for this case. So either: 1. The test needs to be updated (plan missed this) 2. The logic should be different
I'll go with option 1: update the test to reflect the new behavior. The new behavior for this case is: allocate a slot for "new-feature-branch" and navigate to it. The old "old-branch" slot at env.cwd is unaffected.
But the `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment` test was specifically testing slot assignment updates. With the new behavior, the test should verify that a NEW slot assignment is created for "new-feature-branch" and the old one for "old-branch" at env.cwd remains.
Actually wait - let me check what happens in the NEW behavior for this test: 1. `find_worktree_for_branch("new-feature-branch")` → None 2. `current_branch = "old-branch"` ≠ "new-feature-branch" 3. → `already_had_worktree = False` 4. In `_execute_teleport`: `ensure_branch_has_worktree(...)` is called 5. `ensure_branch_has_worktree` → `find_worktree_for_branch` → None → `allocate_slot_for_branch` 6. `allocate_slot_for_branch` creates a new worktree at some path with slot assignment for "new-feature-branch"
After execution: - Pool state has: original slot for "old-branch" at env.cwd + new slot for "new-feature-branch" at new path - 2 assignments total
So for the test to reflect new behavior, it should check: - 2 assignments total (or just check the new one) - New assignment for "new-feature-branch" exists
But this test was called `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment`. The name and intent are different from the new behavior. It should probably be renamed or repurposed.
Given the plan doesn't mention updating this test, let me check: maybe I should look at what EXACTLY the plan says about the behavior change and whether there's a scenario where `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment` is still relevant.
Actually, looking at the comment in the plan:
> `_execute_in_place_teleport()` → single `_execute_teleport()` that: > - If branch was NOT already in a worktree, navigates to the newly allocated slot
"Navigates to the newly allocated slot" - this means we move FROM the current worktree TO the new slot. For `test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment`, the test would now see us navigate to the new slot for "new-feature-branch" rather than staying at env.cwd.
I think the plan DOES intend for this behavior change, and the test should be updated. Let me update it to test the new behavior:
Actually, I realize this test (`test_teleport_in_place_updates_slot_assignment`) was specifically testing an edge case of the OLD in-place behavior (updating slot at current worktree to new branch). With the new unified behavior, this case now allocates a new slot. The OLD slot is simply deactivated (not deleted).

Now let me think about **Problems 6 and 7** (dry-run tests). For `test_teleport_dry_run_in_place_shows_local_state` and `test_teleport_dry_run_in_place_already_in_sync`:
Both tests have `current_branches={env.cwd: "feature-branch"}` - they're ON the target branch. With my updated code, `current_branch == branch_name` → `already_had_worktree=True`. So these should work correctly now.

**Failures:**
1. `tests/commands/pr/test_teleport.py::test_teleport_script_mode_with_sync_includes_gt_submit` (line 386) - AssertionError: Script output is empty when it should contain content - Expected: `script_content.strip() != ""` - Got: empty string
2. `tests/commands/pr/test_teleport.py::test_teleport_script_mode_without_sync_omits_gt_submit` (line 415) - AssertionError: Script output is empty when it should contain content - Expected: `script_content.strip() != ""` - Got: empty string
Both failures are related to `--script` mode producing empty output instead of the expected activation script content. agentId: ae2a015 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)
session.assistant: Good progress! Down to 2 failures. Let me look at those two tests to understand what's happening with the script mode:
session.tool_result:
359→def test_teleport_script_mode_with_sync_includes_gt_submit() -> None: 360→ """Test teleport --script --sync includes gt submit in activation script. 361→ 362→ When teleporting with --script and --sync, the activation script should 363→ contain 'gt submit --no-interactive' as a post-cd command. 364→ """ 365→ runner = CliRunner() 366→ with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env: 367→ env.setup_repo_structure() 368→ pr = _make_pr_details(200, "feature-sync") 369→ github = FakeLocalGitHub(pr_details={200: pr}) 370→ git = FakeGit( 371→ git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir}, 372→ default_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, 373→ trunk_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, 374→ local_branches={env.cwd: ["main", "feature-sync"]}, 375→ remote_branches={env.cwd: ["origin/main", "origin/feature-sync"]}, 376→ existing_paths={env.cwd, env.repo.worktrees_dir}, 377→ ) 378→ ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, git=git, github=github, use_graphite=True) 379→ 380→ result = runner.invoke( 381→ slot_group, ["teleport", "200", "--script", "--sync"], obj=ctx 382→ ) 383→ 384→ assert result.exit_code == 0 385→ script_content = result.stdout 386→ assert script_content.strip() != "" 387→ assert "gt submit --no-interactive" in script_content 388→
... [45 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: For `test_teleport_script_mode_with_sync_includes_gt_submit`: - No `current_branches` set → defaults to... what? Let me check FakeGit. - No `worktrees` configured → `find_worktree_for_branch` returns None - No `current_branches` configured → `get_current_branch(cwd)` returns... ?
session.tool_result: 316: def get_current_branch(self, cwd: Path) -> str | None: 317- """Get the currently checked-out branch.""" 318- return self._current_branches.get(cwd) 319- 320- def list_local_branches(self, repo_root: Path) -> list[str]: 321- """List all local branch names in the repository.""" 322- return self._local_branches.get(repo_root, []) 323- 324- def list_remote_branches(self, repo_root: Path) -> list[str]: 325- """List all remote branch names in the repository.""" 326- return self._remote_branches.get(repo_root, [])
session.assistant:
`get_current_branch` returns `None` if `current_branches` is not configured. So `current_branch = None` ≠ `"feature-sync"`.
So for the test: - `find_worktree_for_branch` → None (no worktrees) - `current_branch = None` ≠ `"feature-sync"` - → `already_had_worktree = False`
In `_execute_teleport` with `already_had_worktree=False`: - calls `ensure_branch_has_worktree(ctx, repo, branch_name="feature-sync", no_slot=False, force=force)` - Inside `ensure_branch_has_worktree`: - `find_worktree_for_branch` → None - calls `allocate_slot_for_branch(...)` which creates a slot in `env.repo.worktrees_dir`
The test has `existing_paths={env.cwd, env.repo.worktrees_dir}` and `env.setup_repo_structure()`. This sets up the repo so `worktrees_dir` exists. Let me check if `allocate_slot_for_branch` works in test context with the fake git.
The issue might be that `allocate_slot_for_branch` does complex pool management and might fail or produce no output.
Actually, looking at what the OLD tests were doing: these tests had `--new-slot` and the old `_execute_new_slot_teleport` was called which called `_fetch_and_update_branch` then `ensure_branch_has_worktree`.
With `feature-sync` in `local_branches` and `remote_branches`, `_fetch_and_update_branch` would: - `remote_ref = "origin/feature-sync"` IS in remote_branches ✓ - `branch_name = "feature-sync"` IS in local_branches - → force-update: `create_branch(repo.root, "feature-sync", "origin/feature-sync", force=True)`
Then `ensure_branch_has_worktree` → `find_worktree_for_branch` → None → `allocate_slot_for_branch`.
The output was the activation script. The old test passed, so `allocate_slot_for_branch` + `navigate_and_display_checkout` in script mode DID produce output.
Now in my new `_execute_teleport` (for `already_had_worktree=False`): 1. `ensure_branch_has_worktree` → allocate slot at some path (let's call it `slot_path`) 2. `_confirm_overwrite` - `branch_exists_locally=True`, `force=False` (no --force passed) → calls `_confirm_overwrite`
Wait! The tests pass `--script --sync` but NOT `--force`. And `branch_exists_locally = True` (feature-sync IS in local_branches). So `_confirm_overwrite` would be called, which shows a prompt.
In CliRunner tests, `click.confirm` would fail (no interactive input) and exit! That's why the output is empty - the command is prompting for confirmation but there's no input.
In the OLD code, `_execute_new_slot_teleport` (the `--new-slot` path) did NOT call `_confirm_overwrite`. Let me check:
Looking at the old `_execute_new_slot_teleport`: ```python def _execute_new_slot_teleport(...): _fetch_and_update_branch(...) _register_with_graphite(...) worktree_path, _ = ensure_branch_has_worktree(...) ...navigate_and_display_checkout(...) ...print_activation_instructions(...) ```
No `_confirm_overwrite`! The old `--new-slot` path never confirmed. And the old in-place path confirmed only when `branch_exists_locally`.
In my new unified `_execute_teleport`, I call `_confirm_overwrite` when `branch_exists_locally and not force`. This is wrong for the "new worktree" path - we shouldn't confirm when allocating a new slot, only when overwriting an existing one.
The fix: only call `_confirm_overwrite` when `already_had_worktree=True`. When we're allocating a new slot, we're not overwriting an existing worktree, so no confirmation needed.
